mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in quarterly GDP (GDPC1) indicates strong economic growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 70%+ of quarters with GDP growth above 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5.0% signals tight labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods when UNRATE declines below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a robust labor market, creating positive momentum in equity valuations over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to produce positive returns in 50%+ of co-movement instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weak supply-side capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days due to recessionary expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in 70%+ of periods with INDPRO declines exceeding 2% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) indicate increased demand for residential construction, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through related sector exposure.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative in 60%+ of periods with HOUST growth above 10% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in real sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days through immediate sectoral impact.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative in 50%+ of periods when RSAFS exceeds 405,000 (millions).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust economic expansion, creating positive momentum in equity valuations over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction between GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to produce positive returns in 50%+ of co-movement instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.
